title: Weston native Tim Demirjian wins Hulu’s ‘Love Overboard’
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Weston native Tim Demirjian wins Hulu's dating reality show 'Love Overboard' with partner Gia Aldisert.
The show, produced by BU alum Alex Cooper, features singles on a yacht off Malta seeking love through competitions.
Demirjian and Aldisert won a trip around the world as their prize, which they have yet to take.

title: MBTA to release train tickets for World Cup matches at Boston Stadium
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MBTA will sell train tickets for World Cup matches at Gillette Stadium starting April 8 via the mTicket app.
Fans need same-day World Cup match tickets to buy train tickets, which are nonrefundable and limited in availability.
Round-trip fares to Gillette Stadium for matches could exceed $75, compared to $30 for a recent France vs. Brazil match.

title: 2 Boston bars earn spots on 50 Best’s top 100 U.S. bars list
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: The Wig Shop and Hecate in Boston ranked among top 100 bars in North America, according to 50 Best.
The Wig Shop, at 27 Temple Place, ranked 86th with a wig-themed menu and retro speakeasy style.
Hecate, ranked 88th, is located at 48 Gloucester St. with cocktails inspired by Greek mythology.

title: Mass. man sentenced for online threats to public figures, children, elementary school
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Funwayo Mbilini Nyawo, 37, from Wilbraham, was sentenced to 32 months for threatening officials, children, and a school.
Nyawo pleaded guilty to 13 counts of interstate threatening communications and one count of stalking using interstate commerce.
His threats included violence against public figures, children in Wilbraham and Boston, and police departments in multiple locations.

title: The Liberty Hotel unveils a $75K World Cup ultra-luxe package
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Liberty Hotel in Boston offers a $75,000 World Cup package featuring a presidential suite and helicopter transfers.
Available during Boston World Cup match dates, the package includes premium match seats and autographed jerseys.
The three-night minimum stay requires booking four to five weeks in advance at $25,000 per night.

title: Umpire admits he forgot the count during bizarre Red Sox sequence
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Umpire Mark Wegner forgot the count during the Red Sox's 9-2 loss to the Astros, not calling a clear strikeout.
Houston's Cam Smith erroneously stayed at bat after three strikes, eventually walking due to the counting error.
No team or umpire corrected the mistake, allowing the at-bat to continue for an additional six pitches.

title: Hudson man accused of beating grandmother to death with skateboard, also injuring father and sister
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Devin Dube, 29, is accused of murdering his grandmother, Elaine Dube, 90, with a skateboard in their Hudson home.
Dube also allegedly injured his sister and father; he was arrested after being found hiding in a van.
Dube faces multiple charges, including murder and assault, with his arraignment planned for Wednesday.

title: Former Rev Esmir Bajraktarević scored winning penalty in World Cup playoff
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Esmir Bajraktarević scored the winning penalty for Bosnia and Herzegovina against Italy to qualify for the World Cup.
Bajraktarević, 21, a former New England Revolution player, now plays for PSV Eindhoven in the Netherlands.
Iraq also qualified for the World Cup, defeating Bolivia 2-1, and will play Norway in Boston on June 16.

title: Medford schools to close on Good Friday due to staffing shortages
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Medford schools will close on April 3 due to a district-wide staffing shortage, Superintendent Suzanne Galusi announced.
The closure is prompted by a significant rise in staff absences for religious observances and other reasons.
The school year end schedule is adjusted, adding full days on June 22-23 and early release days on June 24-26.

title: 5 restaurants we can’t wait to try in April
author: Jillian Dara
bullets: Avra Estiatorio, a modern Greek restaurant known for seafood, opens mid-April in Back Bay at 400 Newbury St.
Dalia, a Spanish-inspired wood-fired restaurant, debuts April 2 in South Boston at 429 W Broadway.
La Tavernetta, offering coastal Italian cuisine, to open early to mid-April in East Boston at 45 Lewis St.
